title: Electric vehicle public charging infrastructure statistics: January 2026

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Quarterly statistics on the number of public electric vehicle chargers in the United Kingdom, including summaries by geography and charging power band. Data tables for this publication can be found on the EVCI data tables page . As of February 2026, this publication now includes statistics on the number of public EV chargers ( EVSE ) in the United Kingdom. More information about this addition can be found in the publication and the frequently asked questions document.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Data is sourced from the electric vehicle charging point platform Zapmap . As of May 2026, the electric vehicle public charging infrastructure statistics and the electric vehicle charging device grant scheme statistics publication pages will be merged into a single publication page titled: electric vehicle charging infrastructure statistics. All content from both publications will be retained and the page will be updated with new statistics on a quarterly basis. Statistical practices are regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation ( OSR ). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing us with any comments about how we meet these standards.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Electric vehicle charging infrastructure statistics Email evci.stats@dft.gov.uk
